title: Some impressions on the Terrace of the Leper King - Visit Angkor
highlights: Preah Learn Sdech Kunlung – is a small temple north of Terrace of the Elephants and near the center of Angkor Thom. The temple has a terrace wall with deeply carved nagas, demons and other mythological beings. The so-called “Leper King” Terrace was built just north of the Elephant Terrace. It is named, or rather misnamed, for the seated statue which once occupied the platform and has now been moved to the National Museum in Phnom Penh to be protected. ### Want to know more? title: Terrace of the Leper King - Wikipedia
highlights: King (or Leper King Terrace; Khmer: ព្រះលានស្តេចគម្លង់ Preah Lean Sdach Kumlung) is a statue located in the northwest corner of the Royal Square of Angkor Thom, Cambodia. It was built in the Bayon style under Jayavarman VII, though its modern name is from an 8th-century sculpture discovered at the site. A datable inscription of the 14th-15th century identifies it with  ...    and ruler of the underworld. title: Angkor Thom: Terrace of the Leper King
highlights: Terrace of the Leper King (Terrace of the Leper King) is located in the central parts of Angkor Thoma, north of Elephant Terraces. The name of the Terrace of the Leper King is directly related to the Buddha statue found during the clearing at this  ...    It was built at the end of the XII century, during the reign of Jayavarman VII (Jayavarman VII, ruled from 1181 to  ...    The terrace of the Leper King is a monolithic cylindrical structure with a diameter of 25m and a wall height of 6m. It  ...    The outer gallery of the Terrace of the Leper King contains bas-reliefs with figures of armed men, apsaras, demons and  ...    horizontal rows and represent many figures of marine life (fish, crocodiles), animals (elephants), invariable apsaras and garudas, people, warriors and others. The corners of the gallery in some places are decorated with bas-reliefs of nagas (snakes) and garudas (birds).
